message.content:
Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-1, fixes applied vs main). Venv: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
The file /tmp/pass1_confirm.json is a JSON array of 22 pass-1 findings (each: issue, title, file, sev, problem, suggested_fix).
Constraints (NOT bugs): local-first/data-sovereign, no metered API, engine imports no adapter, lazy optional backends.

You are independent CONFIRMER #1 of 2 for finding INDEX 4 (0-based) in /tmp/pass1_confirm.json.
Read that item (its issue number, problem, suggested_fix, file). Then VERIFY THE APPLIED FIX on the current branch: read the changed source AND its regression test (git diff vs main, or just read the file + tests), and where useful run a PoC against the venv to confirm the original problem is actually resolved.
Return: appropriate = the fix correctly addresses the root cause in a sound way (not a hack/incomplete patch/over-claim); complete = the fix fully resolves the finding AND has a regression test that would catch a regression. Set either false if not, and say exactly what is missing. Echo the issue number in 'issue'.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
{
  "issue": "105",
  "title": "Postgres ConnectionPool is leaked (never closed) when schema bootstrap fails after the pool opens",
  "file": "src/assay_engine/persistence/checkpoint.py",
  "sev": "MEDIUM",
  "problem": "In `get_checkpointer`, the pool is opened and registered (line 182-190), then schema bootstrap runs inside the try (lines 194-210). If Postgres is reachable but bootstrap fails (missing CREATE privileges, advisory-lock error, a `setup()` DDL error, transient connection drop during DDL), the `except Exception` at line 212 builds a redacted message and re-raises `RuntimeError` \u2014 but the already-open pool is NEVER closed. It remains in `_OPEN_POOLS` (so it is only freed at process exit) holding min_size=1 live connection plus a background worker thread. PoC: stubbing `ConnectionPool` so `.connect",
  "suggested_fix": "In the `except` handler (or a `finally`/try-around-bootstrap), close the pool that was opened in this call before re-raising \u2014 e.g. `_safe_close(pool)` and remove it from `_OPEN_POOLS` \u2014 so a failed init does not leak the connection/worker thread it just created."
}

harden/pass-1

content.content:
1	"""Durable run-state checkpointer — local, data-sovereign (ADR-0003).
2	
3	The orchestration graph persists its full state (current phase, gate decisions, locked
4	hypotheses, verdicts) through a checkpointer so a run can interrupt at a human-approval gate
5	and resume later. That state holds the entire analysis, so the store is **on-box only**
6	(ADR-0003); a SaaS checkpointer is disqualified.
7	
8	This module is the LangGraph checkpointer factory, ported faithfully from the prior
9	platform's hardened implementation and generalized (dataset-agnostic ``ASSAY_*`` env, no
10	config-file coupling — see ADR-0006). The hardened behaviors are deliberately preserved
11	because each prevents a real production failure:
12	
13	- a **self-healing ConnectionPool** (not a raw connection): when Postgres goes away — node
14	  sleep, restart, idle timeout, a network blip — a cached raw connection is permanently
15	  broken and every later checkpoint op raises, i.e. durability is lost exactly during the
16	  interrupt/resume the checkpointer exists to support. The pool runs a liveness check on
17	  checkout and replaces dead connections;
18	- a **session advisory lock** serializing first-time schema setup across *processes*, run on
19	  the *same* connection as the DDL (a pool would otherwise check out a different connection,
20	  leaving the lock ineffective);
21	- a **once-per-process-per-conn guard** (``setup()`` is idempotent but re-locking + replaying
22	  the migration query on every call is wasteful), with an in-process lock making the
23	  check-and-set atomic so concurrent threads don't both run setup;
24	- **credential redaction**: a backend exception (psycopg/psycopg_pool) can embed the full
25	  ``user:password`` URI/DSN in its own message, so the conn string is scrubbed precisely and
26	  the exception text via URI-userinfo + DSN-password backstops; the redacted message is then
27	  raised *outside* the exception handler, so the credential-bearing original is retained as
28	  neither ``__cause__`` nor ``__context__`` (a logger walking ``__context__`` cannot
29	  re-expose it);
30	- a single shared **atexit** pool-cleanup handler (one per process, not one per call) so the
31	  pool's worker thread is joined before interpreter finalization.
32	
33	LangGraph/psycopg are optional (the ``persistence`` extra) and imported lazily; the
34	credential-redaction and connection-resolution logic is pure and unit-tested offline.
35	"""
36	
37	from __future__ import annotations
38	
39	import atexit
40	import os
41	import re
42	import threading
43	from typing import Any, cast
44	from urllib.parse import urlsplit
45	
46	from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
47	
48	_DEFAULT_PG = "postgresql://localhost:5432/assay"
49	
50	# Arbitrary fixed key for the Postgres session advisory lock that serializes checkpoint
51	# schema migrations across concurrent initializers. The value is irrelevant beyond being a
52	# stable per-logical-schema constant; "ASSY" as an int.
53	_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY = 0x4153_5359
54	
55	# Guards the process-local one-time-init bookkeeping. The PG advisory lock serializes DDL
56	# across PROCESSES; this lock makes the in-process check-and-set sequences (atexit
57	# registration, the initialized-conn set) atomic so two threads can't double-register or
58	# double-setup.
59	_init_lock = threading.Lock()
60	
61	# Pools opened by the factory, closed once at process exit via a single shared atexit handler
62	# (registering one handler per call would accumulate handlers across repeated factory calls).
63	_OPEN_POOLS: list[Any] = []
64	_atexit_registered = False
65	
66	# conn_strs whose schema bootstrap (advisory lock + setup() DDL) has already run in this
67	# process — bootstrap once per process per schema; the advisory lock still guards the
68	# first-time cross-process race.
69	_INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS: set[str] = set()
70	
71	# Strips ``://user:password@`` (URI userinfo) from any URI-like substring. '/' is allowed
72	# inside the userinfo so a password containing '/' is still redacted; only '@' (the userinfo
73	# terminator) and whitespace bound the match.
74	_CREDS_RE = re.compile(r"://[^@\s]+@")
75	# Strips a libpq keyword/value DSN password token (``password=...`` / ``pgpassword=...``),
76	# handling quoted values that may contain spaces.
77	_DSN_PW_RE = re.compile(r"(?i)\b(password|pgpassword)\s*=\s*('[^']*'|\"[^\"]*\"|\S+)")
78	
79	
80	def _sanitize_conn_str(conn_str: str) -> str:
81	    """Return ``scheme://host[:port]/path`` with any ``user:password`` userinfo stripped.
82	
83	    Robust to malformed userinfo: a password containing ``/``/``#`` makes ``urlsplit``'s
84	    ``.port`` raise ``ValueError`` on access, so the whole parse (including attribute access)
85	    is guarded and falls back to the generic userinfo-strip regex / a constant placeholder.
86	    """
87	    try:
88	        u = urlsplit(conn_str)
89	        host = u.hostname or ""
90	        port = u.port
91	        scheme = u.scheme
92	        path = u.path
93	    except ValueError:
94	        return _CREDS_RE.sub("://", conn_str)
95	    if not host:
96	        return _CREDS_RE.sub("://", conn_str) if scheme else "<store>"
97	    if ":" in host:  # IPv6 literal — urlsplit drops the brackets; restore them
98	        host = f"[{host}]"
99	    netloc = f"{host}:{port}" if port else host
100	    return f"{scheme}://{netloc}{path}" if scheme else "<store>"
101	
102	
103	def redact_creds(text: str, conn_str: str = "") -> str:
104	    """Remove credentials a backend exception may embed in its message text.
105	
106	    Layers, in order of precision:
107	
108	    1. exact replacement of the known ``conn_str`` (handles any password characters, incl.
109	       ``/`` and spaces) — this is the precise layer and covers the real connection string;
110	    2. a generic URI-userinfo strip (``://user:pass@`` → ``://``) as a backstop for any
111	       *other* URI in the text;
112	    3. a libpq DSN ``password=``/``pgpassword=`` strip for the keyword/value form.
113	
114	    Layers 2–3 are best-effort for *secondary* endpoints (e.g. a replica DSN) the caller did
115	    not pass as ``conn_str``; the primary connection string is always scrubbed precisely.
116	    """
117	    if conn_str:
118	        text = text.replace(conn_str, _sanitize_conn_str(conn_str))
119	    text = _CREDS_RE.sub("://", text)
120	    return _DSN_PW_RE.sub(r"\1=***", text)
121	
122	
123	def get_postgres_connection_string() -> str:
124	    """Resolve the Postgres connection string (``ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL`` env → loopback default).
125	
126	    The env path bypasses any external config validation, so loopback is enforced here at the
127	    point of use (ADR-0003 defense-in-depth): graph state must not leave the box.
128	    """
129	    url = os.environ.get("ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL") or _DEFAULT_PG
130	    return require_local_uri(url, what="checkpointer connection")
131	
132	
133	def _safe_close(pool: Any) -> None:
134	    """Close one pool, swallowing errors so a single failing ``close()`` does not abort
135	    cleanup of the remaining pools at interpreter exit (``close()`` calls a C ``finish()``
136	    that can raise)."""
137	    try:
138	        pool.close()
139	    except Exception:
140	        pass
141	
142	
143	def _close_all_pools() -> None:
144	    for p in _OPEN_POOLS:
145	        _safe_close(p)
146	
147	
148	def _register_pool_cleanup(pool: Any) -> None:
149	    global _atexit_registered
150	    with _init_lock:
151	        _OPEN_POOLS.append(pool)
152	        if not _atexit_registered:
153	            atexit.register(_close_all_pools)
154	            _atexit_registered = True
155	
156	
157	def get_checkpointer(use_memory: bool = False) -> Any:
158	    """Return a configured LangGraph checkpointer.
159	
160	    ``use_memory=True`` returns an in-memory saver (tests / no-Postgres). Otherwise a
161	    Postgres-backed saver over a self-healing pool, with advisory-locked one-time schema
162	    setup. Raises ``RuntimeError`` (with credentials redacted) if the backend is unreachable
163	    or the extra is not installed.
164	    """
165	    try:
166	        if use_memory:
167	            from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver
168	
169	            return MemorySaver()
170	        from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres import PostgresSaver
171	        from psycopg.rows import dict_row
172	        from psycopg_pool import ConnectionPool
173	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
174	        raise RuntimeError(
175	            "Postgres checkpointer requires the 'persistence' extra "
176	            "(langgraph-checkpoint-postgres, psycopg[binary,pool]) — not installed"
177	        ) from exc
178	
179	    conn_str = get_postgres_connection_string()
180	    failure: str | None = None
181	    pool: Any = None
182	    try:
183	        pool = ConnectionPool(
184	            conn_str,
185	            min_size=1,
186	            max_size=8,
187	            kwargs={"autocommit": True, "prepare_threshold": 0, "row_factory": dict_row},
188	            check=ConnectionPool.check_connection,  # liveness check on checkout (self-healing)
189	            open=True,
190	        )
191	        _register_pool_cleanup(pool)
192	        # Bootstrap the schema once per process per conn_str. The advisory lock and the DDL
193	        # must run on the SAME connection (pg_advisory_lock is session-scoped); a pool-backed
194	        # setup() would lock one connection and DDL on another, leaving the lock ineffective.
195	        with _init_lock:
196	            if conn_str not in _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS:
197	                with pool.connection() as conn:
198	                    conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_lock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
199	                    try:
200	                        # row_factory=dict_row makes this connection dict-rowed at runtime
201	                        # (what PostgresSaver requires); the cast asserts that runtime truth
202	                        # the type system can't infer from the pool kwargs.
203	                        PostgresSaver(cast(Any, conn)).setup()  # DDL on the locked connection
204	                    finally:
205	                        # Best-effort unlock; the lock auto-releases when the connection
206	                        # returns to the pool, and raising here would mask a setup() error.
207	                        try:
208	                            conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_unlock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
209	                        except Exception:
210	                            pass
211	                _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS.add(conn_str)
212	        return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, pool))  # dict-rowed pool (row_factory=dict_row)
213	    except Exception as exc:
214	        # Build the redacted message here, but raise it OUTSIDE the handler (below) so the
215	        # original — credential-bearing — exception is retained as neither __cause__ NOR
216	        # __context__ (a logger walking __context__ would otherwise re-expose the password).
217	        failure = (
218	            f"Postgres checkpointer connection failed (is Postgres running on "
219	            f"{_sanitize_conn_str(conn_str)}?): {redact_creds(str(exc), conn_str)}. "
220	            "For tests without Postgres, use get_checkpointer(use_memory=True)."
221	        )
222	        # The pool may have opened before bootstrap failed — close it and drop it from the
223	        # cleanup registry so a failed init does not leak the connection/worker thread (#105).
224	        if pool is not None:
225	            _safe_close(pool)
226	            with _init_lock:
227	                if pool in _OPEN_POOLS:
228	                    _OPEN_POOLS.remove(pool)
229	    raise RuntimeError(failure)
230	
231	
232	def configured_checkpointer(use_memory: bool | None = None) -> Any:
233	    """Return the checkpointer the environment selects.
234	
235	    ``use_memory`` (or ``ASSAY_CHECKPOINT_BACKEND=memory``) chooses the in-memory saver;
236	    otherwise Postgres.
237	    """
238	    if use_memory is None:
239	        use_memory = os.environ.get("ASSAY_CHECKPOINT_BACKEND", "postgres").lower() == "memory"
240	    return get_checkpointer(use_memory=use_memory)
241

diff --git a/src/assay_engine/persistence/checkpoint.py b/src/assay_engine/persistence/checkpoint.py
index b3104bf..c9841db 100644
--- a/src/assay_engine/persistence/checkpoint.py
+++ b/src/assay_engine/persistence/checkpoint.py
@@ -178,6 +178,7 @@ def get_checkpointer(use_memory: bool = False) -> Any:
 
     conn_str = get_postgres_connection_string()
     failure: str | None = None
+    pool: Any = None
     try:
         pool = ConnectionPool(
             conn_str,
@@ -218,6 +219,13 @@ def get_checkpointer(use_memory: bool = False) -> Any:
             f"{_sanitize_conn_str(conn_str)}?): {redact_creds(str(exc), conn_str)}. "
             "For tests without Postgres, use get_checkpointer(use_memory=True)."
         )
+        # The pool may have opened before bootstrap failed — close it and drop it from the
+        # cleanup registry so a failed init does not leak the connection/worker thread (#105).
+        if pool is not None:
+            _safe_close(pool)
+            with _init_lock:
+                if pool in _OPEN_POOLS:
+                    _OPEN_POOLS.remove(pool)
     raise RuntimeError(failure)

278:            self.closed = False

292:    store.close()
293:    assert fc.closed is False  # #117: an INJECTED client is the caller's to close, not ours
302:    offline, and reset the module's process-local bootstrap state."""

360:    # reset process-local bootstrap bookkeeping
362:    monkeypatch.setattr(cp, "_OPEN_POOLS", [])

393:    assert len(cp._OPEN_POOLS) == 2

399:    # from the cleanup registry — not leaked.

content.text: There is a dedicated regression test `test_pool_closed_and_unregistered_when_bootstrap_fails`. Let me read it in full along with the `fake_pg` fixture to confirm it actually exercises the leak scenario.

content.content:
296	# ---- hardened checkpointer invariants (fake backends injected, no Postgres) ----
297	
298	
299\[REDACTED]
300	def fake_pg(monkeypatch):
301	    """Inject fake langgraph/psycopg modules so get_checkpointer runs the full hardened path
302	    offline, and reset the module's process-local bootstrap state."""
303	    import sys
304	    import types
305	
306	    from assay_engine.persistence import checkpoint as cp
307	
308	    state = {"setup_on": [], "lock_on": [], "pools": [], "atexit_registered": 0}
309	
310	    class FakeConn:
311	        def execute(self, sql, params=None):
312	            if "pg_advisory_lock" in sql:
313	                state["lock_on"].append(id(self))
314	
315	    class FakePool:
316	        check_connection = staticmethod(lambda *a, **k: None)
317	
318	        def __init__(self, conn_str, **kw):
319	            self.conn_str = conn_str
320	            self._conn = FakeConn()
321	            state["pools"].append(self)
322	
323	        def connection(self):
324	            conn = self._conn
325	
326	            class _Ctx:
327	                def __enter__(self_):
328	                    return conn
329	
330	                def __exit__(self_, *a):
331	                    return False
332	
333	            return _Ctx()
334	
335	        def close(self):
336	            pass
337	
338	    class FakeSaver:
339	        def __init__(self, arg):
340	            self.arg = arg
341	
342	        def setup(self):
343	            state["setup_on"].append(id(self.arg))
344	
345	    def _mod(name, **attrs):
346	        m = types.ModuleType(name)
347	        for k, v in attrs.items():
348	            setattr(m, k, v)
349	        monkeypatch.setitem(sys.modules, name, m)
350	        return m
351	
352	    _mod("langgraph")
353	    _mod("langgraph.checkpoint")
354	    _mod("langgraph.checkpoint.postgres", PostgresSaver=FakeSaver)
355	    _mod("langgraph.checkpoint.memory", MemorySaver=type("MemorySaver", (), {}))
356	    _mod("psycopg")
357	    _mod("psycopg.rows", dict_row=object())
358	    _mod("psycopg_pool", ConnectionPool=FakePool)
359	
360	    # reset process-local bootstrap bookkeeping
361	    monkeypatch.setattr(cp, "_INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS", set())
362	    monkeypatch.setattr(cp, "_OPEN_POOLS", [])
363	    monkeypatch.setattr(cp, "_atexit_registered", False)
364	    monkeypatch.setattr(
365	        cp,
366	        "atexit",
367	        types.SimpleNamespace(
368	            register=lambda fn: state.__setitem__(
369	                "atexit_registered", state["atexit_registered"] + 1
370	            )
371	        ),
372	    )
373	    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL", "postgresql://localhost:5432/assay")
374	    return cp, state
375	
376	
377	def test_setup_runs_once_per_conn_and_on_locked_connection(fake_pg):
378	    cp, state = fake_pg
379	    cp.get_checkpointer()
380	    cp.get_checkpointer()  # second call, same conn_str
381	    # setup() ran exactly once despite two factory calls (once-per-conn guard)
382	    assert len(state["setup_on"]) == 1
383	    # the advisory lock and the DDL ran on the SAME connection object
384	    assert state["lock_on"] == state["setup_on"]
385	
386	
387	def test_atexit_pool_cleanup_registered_once(fake_pg):
388	    cp, state = fake_pg
389	    cp.get_checkpointer()
390	    cp.get_checkpointer()
391	    # two pools opened and tracked, but only one shared atexit handler registered
392	    assert len(state["pools"]) == 2
393	    assert len(cp._OPEN_POOLS) == 2
394	    assert state["atexit_registered"] == 1
395	
396	
397	def test_pool_closed_and_unregistered_when_bootstrap_fails(fake_pg):
398	    # #105: if schema bootstrap fails AFTER the pool opened, the pool must be closed and dropped
399	    # from the cleanup registry — not leaked.
400	    import sys
401	
402	    cp, state = fake_pg
403	    closed: list = []
404	    Pool = sys.modules["psycopg_pool"].ConnectionPool
405	    orig_close = Pool.close
406	    Pool.close = lambda self: (closed.append(self), orig_close(self))[1]  # record close
407	    Saver = sys.modules["langgraph.checkpoint.postgres"].PostgresSaver
408	    orig_setup = Saver.setup
409	
410	    def boom(self):
411	        raise RuntimeError("setup failed (schema DDL)")
412	
413	    Saver.setup = boom
414	    try:
415	        with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="connection failed"):
416	            cp.get_checkpointer()
417	        assert len(state["pools"]) == 1  # a pool was opened
418	        assert closed and closed[0] is state["pools"][0]  # and it was closed
419	        assert cp._OPEN_POOLS == []  # and removed from the cleanup registry (no leak)
420	    finally:
421	        Pool.close = orig_close
422	        Saver.setup = orig_setup
423	
424	
425	def test_connection_failure_redacts_and_leaks_no_context(fake_pg, monkeypatch):

content.text:
File restored cleanly (no diff vs the committed branch state). 

My verification is complete. The fix for issue #105 correctly addresses the root cause: when schema bootstrap fails after the pool opens, the `except` handler now calls `_safe_close(pool)` and removes it from `_OPEN_POOLS` under `_init_lock`. The `pool: Any = None` initializer (added) plus the `if pool is not None` guard correctly handles the case where `ConnectionPool()` itself raises (pool never assigned, nothing to close).

input.reasoning: The fix targets the exact root cause described in the finding. In get_checkpointer (src/assay_engine/persistence/checkpoint.py), the pool is opened and registered in _OPEN_POOLS before schema bootstrap (advisory lock + PostgresSaver.setup() DDL) runs inside the same try. If bootstrap fails after the pool opens, the except handler now (lines 222-228) calls _safe_close(pool) and removes the pool from _OPEN_POOLS under _init_lock before re-raising RuntimeError, so the failed init no longer leaks the connection/worker thread. The added `pool: Any = None` initializer plus the `if pool is not None` guard correctly handle the case where ConnectionPool() itself raises (pool unassigned, nothing to close) — no NameError, no stale entry. The locking, redaction-outside-handler, and raise-outside-handler behaviors are preserved; this is not a hack or over-claim. The remove is done under the same _init_lock used by _register_pool_cleanup, so registry mutation is consistent. There is a dedicated regression test, test_pool_closed_and_unregistered_when_bootstrap_fails, that injects a setup() that raises ('boom'), asserts the pool was opened, that it was closed (closed[0] is the opened pool), and that _OPEN_POOLS == [] (no leak). I verified the test passes on the branch, and — by temporarily removing the fix block — that the test fails without it (the close assertion fails), confirming it would catch a regression. Source file restored to committed state afterward (no residual diff).
input.evidence: src/assay_engine/persistence/checkpoint.py lines 181 (pool: Any = None), 222-228 (except: _safe_close(pool) + remove from _OPEN_POOLS under _init_lock); regression test tests/test_persistence.py::test_pool_closed_and_unregistered_when_bootstrap_fails (lines 397-422) with fake_pg fixture (lines 299-374). Ran: .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_persistence.py -k 'bootstrap_fails or atexit_pool or runs_once' -> 3 passed. Mutation check: removing the fix block makes the test FAIL at line 418 (assert closed... -> assert ([])), proving regression coverage; file then restored (git diff --stat empty).

content.text: Verification complete for issue #105. The fix is both appropriate and complete — it closes and unregisters the leaked pool on bootstrap failure, handles the pool-never-opened case, and has a strong regression test that I confirmed fails without the fix.
